review_english: I don't understand how such broken mechanics are allowed in such a fluid game. The sound system is disgusting, people are jumping on your head and using their ultimates, the sounds come from far away or sometimes almost not. After you get shot, you realize that the man came to you. I don't know how many times my friend and I looked at each other because the voices cannot be understood. The movement thing is already pretty messed up. If you are not playing a skirmisher or a flying and jumping character, get well soon, the rabid dog.... they just chase you as soon as they see you. D You know, if you are looking for a bit of tactics, it depends on the characteristics of the characters or the map design, do not expect it at all. D Get a skirmisher character and run at him, slide and shoot him with hipfire guns, or if the man's edpi is not 10k or so because you have already skied 100 meters, he will not be able to hit you with his bullets. D LET'S COME TO THE BULLETS IN THE GAME PATTERN AND ARM SUPPORT. WHAT ABOUT FRIENDS, ARCH PLAYERS ARE DISABLED INDIVIDUALS, ARE WE TRYING TO MAKE THEM HAPPY? WHY DO WE OFFER THESE GUYS AN ARM SUPPORT AT A LEVEL THAT WILL DETACH ALL BULLETS? Regardless of the weapon, these guys have a hit rate of less than 80 bullets. (If you are playing skirmisher and skiing 100 meters, this hit rate may decrease) or after hitting 1-2 bullets, what is the pointless retention rate of the remaining bullets? As soon as I see the man, I open my binoculars and fall to the ground. When will I fight the men hand to hand? Are you kidding? Will the man be knocked down for 1.5-2 seconds? Well, of course, this is possible if you play with the controller and use aim support. Do you play with the D mouse? The game has a lot of .... .... The player base is already a bunch of mindless people, armor, health, bullets. Using the features is futile. Once the man is seen, they just start chasing and since the hitboxes of the skirmisher characters are extremely large (!) and we are playing with arms (!), we have very enjoyable battles). There are problems with everything, from movement mechanics to sound mechanics, from character designs (features, hitbox, etc.) to the accuracy of weapons and bullets in the game. Its potential to be a very good, enjoyable, fluid and smooth battle royale game is decreasing day by day. So, how do so many players play? After playing the game, you will see that only ARM PLAYERS, who play without any tactics, who always look similar to each other and play the same characters (a broken skirmisher and 2-3 flying and escaping characters), USING ARM SUPPORTS, are playing. Do yourself a favor and don't waste any time on this ridiculous game (by the way, with 60k hours, I could only unlock 2 characters with my own free will.

review:
Played for a while now, can't recommend this game for a variety of reasons really.
1: for me I've had this rather annoying infinite loading screen bug for multiple seasons and with each update the game will be playable for bout a week or 2 and then back to infinite loading screen again, looked it up and apparently this has been a thing since ASH(Season 11) and probably further back
2: Another friend's PC will just crash, not the game their pc. Checked many many times, still don't know what either causes the infinite loading or pc crashing ( we kind of think its the anti-cheat)
3. Balancing is just not there, you will likely run into the 3 same characters in both Casual and Ranked. It just feels like The Bot Royale where its only 5 teams of actual people and the rest are bots seems genuinely fun and new player friendly.
loved this game, spent many hours with friends just blasting through it ,but with every update somehow making the game slightly more unplayable or just somehow breaking the game and the predatory way they just try to dig into your  wallet with skins costing as much as new game or indie game ($60 for a bundle or $20 for a skin last I remember.) yea, just don't touch this till they get their act together
TLDR: Bugs/Crashes that plague this game since season 11, Not really new player friendly and just absolute corpo greed, but hey the gunplay is nice at least.

review: I love Apex. Now my hours are low on Steam. However, I have played mostly on console and haven't fully crossed over to a PC to play it yet. This game is amazing and should not be slept on.  Apex is a free-to-play, squad-based battle Royale hero shooter.  Each character has a distinct personality, a passive trait, a tactical ability and an ultimate ability. All playable characters (Legends) are divided into five specific classes: Assault, Skirmisher, Recon, Controller and Support. Apex is a battle Royale format: Squads of three (or two) are dropped onto a large island map. Your team must scavenge for weapons, shields, and supplies while fighting off other squads until only one team remains. The map doesn't always have squads and duos. It rotates. However, trios are always there (wish all the options were there all the time). The game has a practice range to learn the guns and items in the game, alongside bots to practice aiming at buildings to get a feel for the game. They have other game modes that help me with my game, like gun run, control and more. There is a comp, but I have never dared lol. I would say apex is more fun with friends. I can play it alone, but for me so much better with friends.

review:
Apex Legends in it's current state is not the greatest, I have a vast amount of hours on console before I got a PC and those hours were purely seasons 2-15 and I can say for the most part the lore was amazing, we were provided with an ample amount of lore that kept many fans as well as I hooked on the game; it gave a sense of direction but compared to the current state of apex we rarely if not never get lore anymore and it's sad to see and it goes to show that the devs don't care about the player base and only the money they make (that's EA for you). Relatively a long standing issue that I am sure many players agree with is that skins cost WAY too much 18$ for a single legendary skin is absurd; here is how I would list prices: Legendary = 800AC, Epic = 500 AC, Rare = 200AC. The gap in crafting materials for skin items is also weird, how does legendary cost 1200 CM but a Epic costs 400? Again with the same format I think the prices with crafting materials should be Legendary = 1000CM, Epic 500CM,  Rare = 200CM and Common = 50CM. The gameplay is still decent however everything feels oversaturated for lack of a better term, you'll either do amazing or absolute garbage and this is because of SBMM and legends are now overly complicated, people should come home from work and play who ever they want against the best and the worst in the same lobby, the devs tend to forget people with lives play this game not just streamers or chuds that play in their moms basement with no job. 

Overall in the games current state I rate it a 3/10
P.S. Apex's life expectancy is lowering every season, do not commit money or time (unless you just care about gameplay and fun.)

R.I.P Titanfall 3

review:
I have 2500 hours in apex on steam and about 2000 hours on other platforms and other accounts. I really like this game! But I agree that the quality and quantity of unique content in apex has decreased significantly over the years. For example, Alter was released in season 21. The next hero, Axel, was released in season 29. A season lasts an average of 3 months, which means we haven't seen a new hero for 24 months. 2 years!!!! I'm very glad that the developers still care about the servers (haha, servers go brrrr-time out), they make rebalances, change maps sometimes, but it's still not enough to attract new players. As a result, only super-loyal players who have been playing the game for years remain in the game. The average skill in the game is increasing, which means that it's less interesting for new players to play even at lower ranks. This further reduces the influx of loyal players. The game could potentially become a second heroes of the storm. I hope the Respawn team can break this vicious cycle. Because we love the apex very much!
Also, I really hope that the developers won't make Apex Legends 2, but will continue to work on their main product, as it is conceptually endless. Maybe it's worth continuing the story of the Titanfall world, rather than creating new skins for $300 and knives that glow brightly in the dark?
Here are some ideas:
They could create something like new 5v5 arenas to take a piece of the CS2 and Valorant players. 
It is possible to return a permanent solo mode, where only bald Wraiths with a kunai will play. 
If you use old developments, you can also return the train, but make it on a new map like Storm Point, such as Beach Express, and make three new colorful skins for Axel, Conduit, and Maggie. 
Or you can create a new map, such as "European Forest" with fir trees, oaks, and change the game rules from "open space for Axel's maneuvers" to "hiding behind a tree trunk," adding a new character with the ability to create a reflection to hide behind it, for example.

review:
Juego bastante a los Battle Royale y uno de los que más horas le metí fue Apex Legends. Entre Steam y Origin debo tener unas 300 horas aproximadamente, y sinceramente puedo decir que fue uno de los juegos que más me costó aprender a jugar “bien”.

A diferencia de otros Battle Royale como Warzone, PUBG o incluso Counter cuando hablamos de shooters competitivos, Apex tiene una movilidad y un gameplay muchísimo más rápido y complicado. El tema de las habilidades, el movimiento, el tiempo para matar y las peleas súper frenéticas hacen que sea bastante difícil dominarlo. De hecho, es de esos juegos que, aunque le metas muchísimas horas, sentís que nunca terminás de aprender del todo. Personalmente es uno de los shooters que jamás pude llegar a dominar aunque sea un poco, y justamente eso también hace que tenga algo especial.

Algo que sí siento es que Apex es un juego prácticamente hecho para jugar con amigos. Obviamente podés jugar solo, pero la experiencia cambia muchísimo cuando entrás con squad y buena comunicación. No es como CS, PUBG o Warzone donde capaz podés carrear más individualmente o jugar tranquilo solo; acá el trabajo en equipo y coordinar habilidades es demasiado importante.

Dentro de los Battle Royale, Apex sigue siendo bastante único comparado a los demás. La movilidad, los personajes y el ritmo de las partidas hacen que se sienta totalmente distinto. Pero también tiene varios puntos negativos. Uno bastante molesto es el hecho de no poder jugar realmente solo dentro del modo principal, porque dependés muchísimo del squad y muchas veces te terminan tocando compañeros que ni hablan o se van apenas los matan.

Y otro problema grande es que hoy en día la mayoría de jugadores son demasiado buenos. Se nota muchísimo que ya casi no hay jugadores nuevos, entonces muchas partidas terminan siendo bastante complicadas incluso para gente que ya tiene horas encima. A veces parece que todos juegan como si estuvieran en ranked competitivo y eso puede hacer que aprender o simplemente relajarse jugando sea medio frustrante.

Aun así, Apex me parece un juegazo y probablemente uno de los Battle Royale más originales que salieron en los últimos años. Tiene una jugabilidad increíble, peleas súper intensas y momentos que posta son muy divertidos cuando jugás con amigos. Es difícil, sí, pero justamente eso también hace que cuando una partida sale bien se sienta muchísimo más satisfactoria.

Nota: 7/10.

review_english: I play Battle Royale a lot and one of the ones I put the most hours into was Apex Legends. Between Steam and Origin I must have approximately 300 hours, and I can honestly say that it was one of the games that was most difficult for me to learn to play well. Unlike other Battle Royales like Warzone, PUBG or even Counter when we talk about competitive shooters, Apex has much faster and more complicated mobility and gameplay. The theme of skills, movement, time to kill and super frantic fights make it quite difficult to master. In fact, it is one of those games that, even if you put many hours into it, you feel like you never fully finish learning. Personally, it is one of the shooters that I could never master even a little, and that is precisely what also makes it have something special. One thing I do feel is that Apex is a game practically made to play with friends. Obviously you can play alone, but the experience changes a lot when you enter with a squad and good communication. It's not like CS, PUBG or Warzone where you can race more individually or play calmly alone, here teamwork and coordinating skills are very important. Within the Battle Royale, Apex is still quite unique compared to the others. The mobility, the characters and the pace of the games make it feel totally different. But it also has several negative points. One that is quite annoying is the fact that you can't really play alone in the main mode, because you depend a lot on the squad and many times you end up with teammates who don't even speak or leave as soon as you kill them. And another big problem is that nowadays most players are too good. It is very noticeable that there are almost no new players, so many games end up being quite complicated even for people who already have hours of experience. Sometimes it seems like everyone is playing as if they are in competitive ranked and that can make learning or just relaxing while playing somewhat frustrating. Even so, Apex seems like a great game to me and probably one of the most original Battle Royales to come out in recent years. It has incredible gameplay, super intense fights and moments that are very fun when you play with friends. It's difficult, yes, but that's exactly what makes it feel much more satisfying when a game goes well. Note 7 10.
